title: Command Parser — MUD Commands
description: Create a MUDParser that handles MUD text commands using string parsing and regex. Supported commands: 'look', 'go <dir>', 'say <msg>', 'tell <player> <msg>', 'take <item>', 'drop <item>', 'inventory', 'who', 'emote <action>', 'attack <target>', and 'help'. The parse(input_str) method returns a (command, args) tuple where command is the command name and args is a dict with the parsed arguments. Unknown commands return ('unknown', {'input': raw_input}).
test_code:
# Tests for Step 4: MUD Command Parser
assert 'MUDParser' in dir(), 'MUDParser class must be defined'

parser = MUDParser()
assert callable(getattr(parser, 'parse', None)), 'MUDParser must have parse method'

# Test look
cmd, args = parser.parse('look')
assert cmd == 'look', f'Expected look, got {cmd}'
assert args == {}, f'look should have empty args, got {args}'

# Test go
cmd, args = parser.parse('go north')
assert cmd == 'go', f'Expected go, got {cmd}'
assert args['direction'] == 'north', f'Expected north, got {args}'

cmd, args = parser.parse('go southeast')
assert cmd == 'go', 'go southeast should parse as go'
assert args['direction'] == 'southeast', f'Expected southeast, got {args}'

# Test say
cmd, args = parser.parse('say Hello everyone!')
assert cmd == 'say', f'Expected say, got {cmd}'
assert args['message'] == 'Hello everyone!', f'Expected message, got {args}'

# Test tell
cmd, args = parser.parse('tell Alice Meet me at the tavern')
assert cmd == 'tell', f'Expected tell, got {cmd}'
assert args['player'] == 'Alice', f'Expected Alice, got {args}'
assert args['message'] == 'Meet me at the tavern', f'Expected message, got {args}'

# Test take
cmd, args = parser.parse('take rusty sword')
assert cmd == 'take', f'Expected take, got {cmd}'
assert args['item'] == 'rusty sword', f'Expected rusty sword, got {args}'

# Test drop
cmd, args = parser.parse('drop gold coin')
assert cmd == 'drop', f'Expected drop, got {cmd}'
assert args['item'] == 'gold coin', f'Expected gold coin, got {args}'

# Test inventory
cmd, args = parser.parse('inventory')
assert cmd == 'inventory', f'Expected inventory, got {cmd}'

# Test who
cmd, args = parser.parse('who')
assert cmd == 'who', f'Expected who, got {cmd}'

# Test emote
cmd, args = parser.parse('emote dances a jig')
assert cmd == 'emote', f'Expected emote, got {cmd}'
assert args['action'] == 'dances a jig', f'Expected action, got {args}'

# Test attack
cmd, args = parser.parse('attack goblin')
assert cmd == 'attack', f'Expected attack, got {cmd}'
assert args['target'] == 'goblin', f'Expected goblin, got {args}'

# Test help
cmd, args = parser.parse('help')
assert cmd == 'help', f'Expected help, got {cmd}'

# Test unknown command
cmd, args = parser.parse('dance wildly')
assert cmd == 'unknown', f'Expected unknown, got {cmd}'
assert 'input' in args, 'Unknown command should include input in args'
assert args['input'] == 'dance wildly', f'Expected original input, got {args}'

# Test whitespace handling
cmd, args = parser.parse('  look  ')
assert cmd == 'look', 'Parser should strip whitespace'

# Test empty input
cmd, args = parser.parse('')
assert cmd == 'unknown', 'Empty input should return unknown'
acceptance_criteria: MUDParser compiles regex patterns for all 11 commands
go, say, tell, take, drop, emote, attack extract their arguments correctly
look, inventory, who, help return empty args dicts
Input is stripped of leading/trailing whitespace before parsing
hints: Compile patterns in __init__: self._patterns = [(name, re.compile(pat, re.IGNORECASE)) for name, pat in self.COMMANDS.items()]
In parse(), strip the input first: cleaned = input_str.strip()
Use match.groupdict() to get named groups as a dict.
Loop through self._patterns and try match = pattern.match(cleaned) on each.
